evidence_reg: the existence of the right to withdraw consent at any time, without affecting the lawfulness of processing based on consent before its withdrawal;
per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA mentions the same right to withdraw consent, but it omits conditions and timing; omission alone is not enough to establish a meaning-changing mismatch.

evidence_reg: the existence of the right to withdraw consent at any time, without affecting the lawfulness of processing based on consent before its withdrawal;
per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA aligns at a high level with the same right mentioned in the REG, but it is too fragmentary to support a clear mismatch beyond omitted detail.

evidence_reg: the data subject withdraws consent on which the processing is based
the obligation to erase personal data without undue delay
per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA states a right to withdraw consent, while the REG concerns erasure obligations triggered by withdrawal; these are related but not the same constraint, so no quote-supported deviation is established.
